title: Scene 0 — Opening Invocation

segments.cue: Deep church bell, single toll, long decay. Organ drone enters as bell decays. ~6.5 seconds total.
segments.director_note: Cathedral opening. The bell announces the space; the drone inhabits it.
PREMONITION. The refrain is planted before it has meaning. Slowest and quietest chorus appearance in the piece. Heavy reverb so the line decays into the drone.
The drone breathes alone. Listener sits inside the cathedral.
The word 'Alien' is the weight. The file is filed by an alien narrator.
Date of the incident. Flat, as a filing stamp.
Typewriter clusters as the title is being typed out.

segments.ssml: <speak>God Bless The United States Of Aliens.</speak>
segments.director_note: The title itself — spoken as a filing stamp, not as rhetoric.
The subtitle, establishing genre: surveillance report.
The typewriter trails into Scene I. The report proper begins.

overall_notes: The opening invocation establishes both registers the piece will alternate between: liturgical (bell, organ, chorus premonition) and surveillance (alien narrator, typewriter, incident report header). The whispered 'Someone else...' plants the refrain before the listener has a frame for it — when Scene III leads into the first full chorus appearance, the listener will have heard it once already.
